what: `perf_a11y_verifier` is NOT a standalone file — it is a declared *capability/behavior within the Verifier subagent* (`.claude/agents/verifier.md`), constituting the fifth verification layer. It is configured to do three things when the Verifier evaluates a slice: (1) run k6/Lighthouse against numeric performance budgets (p95 latency, Core Web Vitals) for NFR coverage items of subtype `performance`, attaching the result as an Evidence_Record; (2) run axe-core requiring zero WCAG-A/AA violations on covered screens for NFR items of subtype `accessibility`; (3) treat UI-screen completeness as a verification target — every declared screen/state (including empty, loading, error) must have a behavi…

open_gaps.gap: Host file does not exist. `.claude/agents/verifier.md` — the sole declared home of perf_a11y_verifier — is absent; `.claude/agents/` contains only `.gitkeep`. The capability has no concrete artifact anywhere in the repo.
open_gaps.fix: Author `.claude/agents/verifier.md` (task 10.3) and ensure task 51 amends THAT file to add the fifth layer. Until verifier.md exists, every citation to 'capability within .claude/agents/verifier.md' (design.md:146) is dangling.

open_gaps.gap: Trigger predicate references a non-existent data field. REQ-VERIFY-007 gates 'WHEN an NFR item of subtype `performance`/`accessibility`', but no `subtype` field exists in the CoverageItem JSON schema (enum is only {functional,NFR,WIRING}) nor in the coverage_items Postgres table. There is no way to mark an NFR item as performance vs accessibility, so the WHEN condition can never be evaluated.
open_gaps.fix: Add an optional `subtype` enum field ({performance,accessibility,...}) to the CoverageItem schema (design.md:322-385) and the coverage_items table (design.md:443-451), and have the Verifier dispatch on it. Without this the event-driven trigger is unrealizable.

open_gaps.gap: Phase tag is self-contradictory (1/3) and the build is unphased. The Component Inventory tags it 'Phase 1/3' (design.md:146,1182) while the host Verifier is a Phase-0 spine component (design.md:154,629); the only build task (51) carries no phase and sits in the late-wave cluster (waves 40-43). A reader cannot tell whether this is built in Phase 0, 1, or 3.
open_gaps.fix: Pick one phase. Given the Verifier is Phase 0 and perf/a11y are not spine-critical, tag the capability Phase 1 cleanly and place task 51 in the Phase-1 section with an explicit dependency on task 10.3.

open_gaps.gap: Phase-0 Verifier authoring task omits the fifth layer. Task 10.3 (write verifier.md) lists only structural/semantic/behavioral/security and stops; it never mentions perf/a11y, so following the tasks in order yields a verifier.md without this capability, and task 51 must retro-edit a file that task 10.3 wrote — but task 51 is worded as 'implement a component in the Verifier' without referencing task 10.3.
open_gaps.fix: Add to task 10.3 a forward-reference '(fifth perf/a11y layer added in task 51)' and reword task 51 to 'amend .claude/agents/verifier.md authored in task 10.3'.

open_gaps.gap: No Property and no Z3 CHECK pin REQ-VERIFY-007/008. Every other substantive requirement is pinned by a Property and/or CHECK; perf/a11y/UI-render has neither. There is no machine-checkable or property-based assertion that this layer runs or gates.
open_gaps.fix: Add a correctness Property (e.g. 'For any NFR item of subtype performance|accessibility, a proven flip requires a perf/a11y Evidence_Record') and a property test under tests/property/; optionally a Z3 CHECK modeling 'NFR-subtype-perf proven ⇒ perf-evidence-present'.

open_gaps.gap: Task 51 mis-cites Property 23. It claims perf_a11y_verifier 'Validates Property 23 line-coverage figures via pytest-cov ... coverage.json' — but Property 23 validates Req 9.6 (line-coverage threshold), wholly distinct from REQ-VERIFY-007/008. This conflates two requirements and leaves perf/a11y with a borrowed, wrong test reference.
open_gaps.fix: Remove the Property 23 / coverage.json clause from task 51 and replace with a dedicated perf/a11y Property and test file (e.g. tests/property/test_perf_a11y.py).

open_gaps.gap: Evidence_Record mapping is unspecified for tool outputs. k6/Lighthouse/axe-core emit reports, not pytest results, yet the Evidence_Record requires test_file/test_name/output_hash/collected_at with additionalProperties:false. How a Lighthouse JSON or axe violation list maps into these four fields (and what counts as test_name) is never defined, so the SubagentStop schema gate (design.md:217) may reject valid perf/a11y evidence.
open_gaps.fix: Define a canonical mapping in design.md: e.g. test_file=path to the k6/Lighthouse/axe runner script, test_name=budget id, output_hash=sha256 of the report artifact, collected_at=run timestamp.

open_gaps.gap: No tool dependencies pinned and no CI wiring. The scaffold task 1 pins z3/hypothesis/jsonschema/psycopg2/semgrep/playwright but NOT k6, Lighthouse, or axe-core; no workflow (coverage-gate.yml or otherwise) runs perf/a11y as a status check. The capability has no path to actually execute in CI.
open_gaps.fix: Add k6/Lighthouse/@axe-core (or playwright-axe) to task 1 dependency pins and add a CI step (or extend coverage-gate.yml) that runs perf/a11y for NFR items, mirroring the Playwright step in task 15.

open_gaps.gap: REQ-VERIFY-008 'declared screen/state' has no data source. UI-screen completeness requires knowing which screens/states are 'declared in the coverage model', but no schema field enumerates screens/states (no `screens`, `states`, or UI-declaration structure in feature_list.json schema). The Ubiquitous obligation cannot be checked against an undefined declaration set.
open_gaps.fix: Add a screen/state declaration structure to the coverage model (e.g. a WIRING/NFR item subtype 'ui-screen' carrying declared states) so 'every declared screen/state' is enumerable and the render assertion is enforceable.

open_gaps.gap: design.md/tasks.md coverage of this component is thin. Across all three canonical files the capability appears in exactly 4 design.md lines (146, 255, 262, 1182), 1 tasks.md line (966), and 3 requirements.md criteria — with no interface signature, no permission delta, no dispatch logic, no CI step, no test, no threshold ownership. This thinness is itself a gap: a Phase-1/3 verification layer is specified almost entirely by a one-line inventory row plus a one-line task.
open_gaps.fix: Add a dedicated design.md subsection (like the Sandbox/Worktree subsection) covering: subtype dispatch, tool invocation, Evidence_Record mapping, thresholds + owner, CI wiring, and the Property/test; expand task 51 into subtasks (51.1 perf, 51.2 a11y, 51.3 UI-render, 51.4 property test).

critic_findings.detail: REQ-VERIFY-008's UI-screen render assertion is a BEHAVIORAL test, and the existing behavioral layer is Playwright (design.md:260 'Runs behavioral checks via Playwright CLI'; Req 9.3 requires a captured behavioral artifact as Evidence_Record). The draft routes UI-render only through k6/Lighthouse/axe-core and never identifies the Playwright behavioral layer (task 15 / design.md:260) as the actual mechanism a render assertion would use. This is a real dependency/wiring relationship present in the docs but absent from the draft's dependencies and behaviors — and it matters because render tests are NOT a perf/a11y-tool concern, so the draft mislocates them.

The WIRING integration-test-evidence obligation (Req 8.3 / task 47) bears directly on REQ-VERIFY-008 and is entirely absent from the draft. The canonical PRD explicitly frames UI-screen completeness as a 'WIRING-adjacent verification target' (PRD line 59). Req 8.3 ('WHEN a WIRING item is claimed proven, THE System SHALL require an integration test exercising the real execution path as the attached Evidence_Record') and its build task 47 are the nearest EXISTING evidence mechanism that could host the screen-render assertion, yet the draft never links them. An obligation that exists in the docs and is relevant to the component is missing from both ears_stories and open_gaps.

The draft's open-gap on 'declared screen/state has no data source' is correct but undersells the strongest evidence. PRD line 59 (the canonical REQ-VERIFY-008 source) states screens are 'declared IN THE COVERAGE MODEL (including empty, loading, and error states)' and calls it 'WIRING-adjacent'. The draft cites requirements.md:449 and design.md:322-385 but never quotes PRD:59's explicit 'declared in the coverage model' clause — which is the load-bearing proof that the Ubiquitous obligation references a coverage-model structure that does not exist (no screens/states field in CoverageItem or the WIRING type). The gap is real; the citation is weaker than the available evidence.

The draft caught the missing `subtype` field but missed a second, independent dispatch defect: the CoverageItem schema's `ears_pattern` enum is {ubiquitous, event-driven, state-driven, unwanted, optional} and `type` is {functional, NFR, WIRING}. There is no field at all on which the Verifier can dispatch performance-vs-accessibility-vs-render — not just `subtype` is absent, but the schema offers no representable discriminator for the three distinct behaviors of this one capability. The draft treats subtype as the single blocker; in fact the schema has zero hooks for any of the three sub-behaviors, so even adding subtype only solves perf-vs-a11y and not the REQ-VERIFY-008 render branch (which…

The draft does not flag that REQ-VERIFY-008 is the ONLY one of the three sub-obligations with NO named tool, NO Evidence_Record-mapping path, and NO subtype value — perf maps to k6/Lighthouse and a11y to axe-core, but UI-render maps to nothing (it is neither a performance nor accessibility NFR subtype, and PRD:59 calls it a separate WIRING-adjacent target). The draft lumps all three under one capability and marks REQ-VERIFY-008 'partial' alongside the others, masking that REQ-VERIFY-008 is materially more unspecified than 007: it has no tool, no subtype, no data source, and no evidence mapping simultaneously.

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHEN an NFR item of subtype `performance` is verified, THE System SHALL run k6/Lighthouse against numeric budgets (p95 latency, Core Web Vitals) and attach the result as the Evidence_Record.
ears_needing_work.how: Capability named in Verifier behaviors (design.md:262) and build task 51 (tasks.md:966). The runtime trigger is 'NFR item of subtype performance'.

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHEN an NFR item of subtype `accessibility` is verified, THE System SHALL run axe-core and require zero WCAG-A/AA violations on covered screens.
ears_needing_work.how: Named in Verifier behaviors and task 51; zero-WCAG-A/AA gate is stated.

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL treat UI-screen completeness as a verification target: every declared screen/state (incl. empty, loading, error) SHALL have a behavioral test asserting it renders.
ears_needing_work.how: Named in REQ-VERIFY-008 (Ubiquitous), Verifier behavior, and task 51's render-test assertion. WIRING-adjacent but no mechanism specifies how screens are 'declared' in the coverage model or how the assertion is enforced.

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHEN a behavioral check runs, THE Verifier SHALL produce a captured artifact attached as an Evidence_Record.
ears_needing_work.how: perf/a11y results are attached as Evidence_Records per REQ-VERIFY-007; reuses evidence_collector.py.

ears_needing_work.criterion: structured record with exactly four required fields test_file/test_name/output_hash/collected_at.
ears_needing_work.how: perf/a11y output must conform to the same four-field schema; k6/Lighthouse/axe outputs would need mapping into test_file/test_name/output_hash — this mapping is unspecified.

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL represent WIRING obligations and NFR items as first-class coverage items in feature_list.json.
ears_needing_work.how: NFR is a first-class type in the schema enum {functional,NFR,WIRING}; perf/a11y items are NFR-typed.

ears_needing_work.criterion: any touched file with line coverage <85% → Verifier marks slice failed, no proven status (via pytest-cov/coverage.json).
ears_needing_work.how: task 51 INCORRECTLY claims perf_a11y_verifier 'Validates Property 23 line-coverage figures' — but Property 23 validates Req 9.6 (coverage threshold), an entirely different concern from REQ-VERIFY-007/008. This is a mis-citation conflating two distinct requirements.

ears_needing_work.criterion: every requirement should be pinned by a correctness Property and/or Z3 CHECK.
ears_needing_work.how: NO Property and NO Z3 CHECK pins REQ-VERIFY-007 or REQ-VERIFY-008. Properties 1-30 contain none for perf/a11y/UI-render; formal_verification_merged.py has no CHECK referencing VERIFY-007/008.

citation_issues: verification/formal_verification_merged.py:1-523 — off-by-one phantom: the file is exactly 522 lines (verified via `wc -l`). Line 523 does not exist. The correct citation range is 1-522. This appears in the 'properties_and_checks' field ("formal_verification_merged.py (34 checks, CHECK-1..13b) has no assertion referencing REQ-VERIFY-007/008; perf/a11y is acknowledged as runtime-only, never formally modeled (verification/formal_verification_merged.py:1-523)") and in the 'dependencies' field. The substantive claim is correct (no VERIFY-007/008 references confirmed by grep), but the end-line number is phantom.
